lines.gurmukhi: ਲਾਗੀ ਭੂਖ ਮਾਇਆ ਮਗੁ ਜੋਹੈ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਪਦਾਰਥੁ ਮੋਹਿ ਖਰੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: laagee bhookh maaeaa mag johai mukat padaarath mohi khare |3|
lines.english: Driven by hunger, it sees the path of Maya's riches; this emotional attachment takes away the treasure of liberation. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਣ ਪਲਾਵ ਕਰੇ ਨਹੀ ਪਾਵੈ ਇਤ ਉਤ ਢੂਢਤ ਥਾਕਿ ਪਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: karan palaav kare nahee paavai it ut dtoodtat thaak pare |
lines.english: Weeping and wailing, he does not receive them; he searches here and there, and grows weary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮਿ ਕ੍ਰੋਧਿ ਅਹੰਕਾਰਿ ਵਿਆਪੇ ਕੂੜ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਸਿਉ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਕਰੇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: kaam krodh ahankaar viaape koorr kuttanb siau preet kare |4|
lines.english: Engrossed in sexual desire, anger and egotism, he falls in love with his false relatives. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਨ ਆਪੁ ਪਛਾਣੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਨ ਕਾਲੁ ਟਰੇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: bin gurasabad na aap pachhaanai bin har naam na kaal ttare |5|
lines.english: Without the Word of the Guru's Shabad, he does not undersand himself. Without the Lord's Name, death cannot be avoided. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਾਵੈ ਭੋਗੈ ਸੁਣਿ ਸੁਣਿ ਦੇਖੈ ਪਹਿਰਿ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਕਾਲ ਘਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: khaavai bhogai sun sun dekhai pahir dikhaavai kaal ghare |
lines.english: He eats and enjoys, listens and watches, and dresses up to show off in this house of death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਬਿਨਸੈ ਸਹਸੈ ਸਹਸਾ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਵੈ ਮੁਖਿ ਧੂਰਿ ਪਰੇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: tan dhan binasai sahasai sahasaa fir pachhutaavai mukh dhoor pare |6|
lines.english: His body and wealth pass away, and he is torn by skepticism and cynicism; in the end, he regrets and repents, when the dust falls on his face. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਤਾ ਮੋਹੁ ਹਉਮੈ ਕਰਿ ਭੂਲੇ ਮੇਰੀ ਮੇਰੀ ਕਰਤੇ ਛੀਨਿ ਖਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: jetaa mohu haumai kar bhoole meree meree karate chheen khare |
lines.english: The more attachment and egotism delude and confuse him, the more he cries out, "Mine, mine!", and the more he loses out.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਰਧਿ ਭਇਆ ਜੋਬਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਖਿਸਿਆ ਕਫੁ ਕੰਠੁ ਬਿਰੂਧੋ ਨੈਨਹੁ ਨੀਰੁ ਢਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: biradh bheaa joban tan khisiaa kaf kantth biroodho nainahu neer dtare |
lines.english: He grows old, his body and youth waste away, and his throat is plugged with mucous; water flows from his eyes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਣ ਰਹੇ ਕਰ ਕੰਪਣ ਲਾਗੇ ਸਾਕਤ ਰਾਮੁ ਨ ਰਿਦੈ ਹਰੇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: charan rahe kar kanpan laage saakat raam na ridai hare |7|
lines.english: He feet fail him, and his hands shake and tremble; the faithless cynic does not enshrine the Lord in his heart. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗਈ ਕਾਲੀ ਹੂ ਧਉਲੇ ਕਿਸੈ ਨ ਭਾਵੈ ਰਖਿਓ ਘਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: surat gee kaalee hoo dhaule kisai na bhaavai rakhio ghare |
lines.english: His intellect fails him, his black hair turns white, and no one wants to keep him in their home.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਸਰਤ ਨਾਮ ਐਸੇ ਦੋਖ ਲਾਗਹਿ ਜਮੁ ਮਾਰਿ ਸਮਾਰੇ ਨਰਕਿ ਖਰੇ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: bisarat naam aise dokh laageh jam maar samaare narak khare |8|
lines.english: Forgetting the Naam, these are the stigmas which stick to him; the Messenger of Death beats him, and drags him to hell. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਬਾਦਿ ਜੀਵਣੁ ਹੋਰੁ ਮਰਣਾ ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰਸਬਦੈ ਜਨਮੁ ਜਰੇ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: bin gur baad jeevan hor maranaa bin gurasabadai janam jare |9|
lines.english: Without the Guru, life and death are pointless; without the Word of the Guru's Shabad, life just burns away. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਬ ਜਨਮ ਕੋ ਲੇਖੁ ਨ ਮਿਟਈ ਜਨਮਿ ਮਰੈ ਕਾ ਕਉ ਦੋਸੁ ਧਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: poorab janam ko lekh na mittee janam marai kaa kau dos dhare |
lines.english: The record of one's past actions cannot be erased; who else is to blame for one's birth and death?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਬਿਸਾਰਿ ਲੋਭਿ ਮੂਲੁ ਖੋਇਓ ਸਿਰਿ ਧਰਮ ਰਾਇ ਕਾ ਡੰਡੁ ਪਰੇ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: naam bisaar lobh mool khoeo sir dharam raae kaa ddandd pare |10|
lines.english: Forgetting the Naam, and caught by greed, he betrays his own source; the club of the Righteous Judge of Dharma will strike him over the head. ||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੁਸੀ ਖੁਆਰ ਭਏ ਰਸ ਭੋਗਣ ਫੋਕਟ ਕਰਮ ਵਿਕਾਰ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: khusee khuaar bhe ras bhogan fokatt karam vikaar kare |
lines.english: The pleasures enjoyed in happiness bring ruin; acting in corruption is useless indulgence.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵਹਿ ਜਾ ਕਉ ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh raam naam gun gaaveh jaa kau har prabh nadar kare |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord's Name; the Lord God blesses them with His Glance of Grace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਿਮਰਹੁ ਗੁਰ ਬਚਨ ਸਮਾਰਹੁ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਭਾਉ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: har simarahu gur bachan samaarahu sangat har jan bhaau kare |
lines.english: Meditate in remembrance on the Lord; meditate and contemplate the Guru's Word, and love to associate with the humble servants of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਪੁਰਖ ਅਪਰੰਪਰ ਪੂਰੇ ਤੇ ਜਗ ਮਹਿ ਗੁਰ ਗੋਵਿੰਦ ਹਰੇ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: te niramal purakh aparanpar poore te jag meh gur govind hare |11|
lines.english: Those beings are pure, perfect unlimited and infinite; in this world, they are the embodiment of the Guru, the Lord of the Universe. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਗੁਰੁ ਪਰਧਾਨੁ ਦੁਆਰੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਤਿਨ ਜਨ ਕੀ ਰੇਣੁ ਹਰੇ ॥੧੨॥੮॥
lines.translit: har jan gur paradhaan duaarai naanak tin jan kee ren hare |12|8|
lines.english: The Lord's humble servants are the embodiment of the Guru; they are supreme and respected in the Court of the Lord. Nanak seeks the dust of the feet of those humble servants of the Lord. ||12||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾ ਧਨ ਢੋਈ ਨ ਲਹੈ ਵਾਢੀ ਕਿਉ ਧੀਰੇਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: saa dhan dtoee na lahai vaadtee kiau dheereo |1|
lines.english: The soul-bride is separated from her Lord, and she has no place of rest; how can she be comforted? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਉ ਵੰਞਉ ਡੁੰਮਣੀ ਕਿਤੀ ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਕਰੇਉ ॥
lines.translit: aavau vanyau ddunmanee kitee mitr kareo |
lines.english: The double-minded person comes and goes, and has numerous friends.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਕਾਫੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਘਰੁ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo kaafee mahalaa 1 ghar 2 |
lines.english: Maaroo, Kaafee, First Mehl, Second House:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈਡਾ ਮਨੁ ਰਤਾ ਆਪਨੜੇ ਪਿਰ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: maiddaa man rataa aapanarre pir naal |
lines.english: My mind is attuned to the Love of my Husband Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਘੋਲਿ ਘੁਮਾਈ ਖੰਨੀਐ ਕੀਤੀ ਹਿਕ ਭੋਰੀ ਨਦਰਿ ਨਿਹਾਲਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: hau ghol ghumaaee khaneeai keetee hik bhoree nadar nihaal |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I am devoted, dedicated, a sacrifice to the Lord; if only He would bless me with His Glance of Grace, even for an instant! ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਗਲਿ ਅਉਗਣ ਮੁਠੜੀ ਬਿਨੁ ਪਿਰ ਝੂਰਿ ਮਰਾਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: mai gal aaugan muttharree bin pir jhoor maraau |2|
lines.english: I wear my faults around my neck; without my Husband Lord, I am grieving, and wasting away to death. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੇਈਅੜੈ ਡੋਹਾਗਣੀ ਸਾਹੁਰੜੈ ਕਿਉ ਜਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: peeearrai ddohaaganee saahurarrai kiau jaau |
lines.english: I am a rejected bride, abandoned in my parents' home; how can I go to my in-laws now?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੇਈਅੜੈ ਪਿਰੁ ਸੰਮਲਾ ਸਾਹੁਰੜੈ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: peeearrai pir samalaa saahurarrai ghar vaas |
lines.english: But if, in my parents' home, I remember my Husband Lord, then I will come to dwell in the home of my in-laws yet.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖਿ ਸਵੰਧਿ ਸੋਹਾਗਣੀ ਪਿਰੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਗੁਣਤਾਸੁ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: sukh savandh sohaaganee pir paaeaa gunataas |3|
lines.english: The happy soul-brides sleep in peace; they find their Husband Lord, the treasure of virtue. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਰੁ ਮੁਤੀ ਡੋਹਾਗਣੀ ਤਿਨ ਡੁਖੀ ਰੈਣਿ ਵਿਹਾਇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: pir mutee ddohaaganee tin ddukhee rain vihaae |4|
lines.english: The Lord rejects the impure soul-brides. Their life-night passes in misery. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੇਫੁ ਨਿਹਾਲੀ ਪਟ ਕੀ ਕਾਪੜੁ ਅੰਗਿ ਬਣਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: lef nihaalee patt kee kaaparr ang banaae |
lines.english: Their blankets and mattresses are made of silk, and so are the clothes on their bodies.
